1.title: Getting Started with Affilabs
1.content:
Affilabs is Affinité Instruments' SPR data acquisition software. To get started:

1. Connect your SPR instrument via USB
2. Launch Affilabs - it will auto-detect your device
3. Run OEM LED Calibration (Settings tab)
4. Start acquiring data using the Start Recording button

For detailed tutorials, visit the Support section on our website.

2.title: SPR Technology Overview
2.content:
Surface Plasmon Resonance (SPR) is a label-free detection method for measuring biomolecular interactions in real-time. Affinité Instruments provides compact, user-friendly SPR systems suitable for research and quality control applications.

Our instruments offer:
• Multi-channel detection (up to 4 channels)
• Real-time kinetic measurements
• Temperature control
• Integrated microfluidics

Learn more about SPR technology on our website.

3.title: Contact Support
3.content:
For technical support, questions, or assistance:

• Email: info@affiniteinstruments.com
• Website: https://www.affiniteinstruments.com/
• Submit questions through our contact form

Our support team typically responds within 1 business day.

4.title: SPR Detector Calibration Guide
4.content:
The SPR detector requires periodic calibration for optimal performance.

**When to Calibrate:**
• After instrument startup (daily)
• When baseline appears unstable
• After changing buffer conditions
• Following maintenance procedures

**Calibration Procedure:**
1. Ensure stable flow (no bubbles)
2. Navigate to Settings → OEM LED Calibration
3. Click 'Start Calibration'
4. Wait 30-60 seconds for completion
5. Review calibration report
6. Click 'Accept' if values are within range

**Expected Values:**
• Wavelength stability: < 0.01 nm RSD
• Intensity variation: < 2%
• Baseline drift: < 5 RU/min

If calibration fails, check for air bubbles, verify flow rate, and ensure temperature stability.

5.title: Multi-Channel SPR Detection
5.content:
Affinité Instruments SPR systems support simultaneous multi-channel detection for parallel sample analysis.

**Available Configurations:**
• 1-channel: Single detection spot
• 2-channel: Dual detection (sample + reference)
• 4-channel: Quad detection for throughput

**Applications:**
• Kinetic comparisons across samples
• Reference-subtracted measurements
• Concentration series screening
• Multiplexed assays

**Channel Selection in Affilabs:**
Use the Flow tab to route samples to specific channels. The valve selector (A/B/C/D) controls which channel receives flow. All active channels are displayed simultaneously on the sensorgram.

6.title: What is Affilabs Software?
6.content:
Affilabs is the official software for Affinité Instruments SPR systems. It provides:

**Main Features:**
• Real-time data visualization
• Automated data collection
• Built-in analysis tools
• Export capabilities (Excel, CSV, images)
• Method automation
• Multi-user support

**System Requirements:**
• Windows 10 or later
• USB port for instrument connection
• 4GB RAM (8GB recommended)
• 2GB free disk space

**No Hardware Needed For:**
• Viewing previous data files
• Learning the interface
• Setting up user profiles
• Exploring analysis features

7.title: Before Connecting Hardware
7.content:
Before connecting your SPR instrument, you can:

**Software Preparation:**
• Install Affilabs software
• Create user accounts
• Explore the interface layout
• Review documentation
• Set up data export folders

**Learning Resources:**
• Built-in help system
• Online user manual
• Video tutorials
• Contact support for questions

**What Requires Hardware:**
• Calibration procedures
• Live data acquisition
• Real-time measurements
• Pump and valve control
• Temperature monitoring

**Tip:** Get familiar with the software interface first to maximize efficiency once hardware is connected.

8.title: Creating Methods in Affilabs
8.content:
Methods in Affilabs are experimental sequences made up of individual cycles.

**Where to Create Methods:**
• Right sidebar → Method tab (looks like a flask icon)
• Click '+ Add Cycle' to build your method step by step

**Basic Method Structure:**
1. **Baseline** (3-5 min) - Establish stable signal
2. **Association** (5-15 min) - Sample injection and binding
3. **Dissociation** (5-30 min) - Watch sample unbind
4. **Regeneration** (1-3 min) - Clean surface for reuse

**Cycle Types Available:**
• Baseline - Stable buffer flow
• Binding - Manual injection, incubation with contact time
• Kinetic - Flow injection, association + dissociation phases
• Regeneration - Surface cleaning
• Wash - System cleaning
• Immobilization - Attach ligand to surface

**Quick Start:**
1. Go to Method tab (right sidebar)
2. Click 'Add Cycle'
3. Choose cycle type from dropdown
4. Set duration (e.g., 5.0 minutes)
5. Name your cycle (e.g., '100nM Sample')
6. Click 'Add' to queue
7. Repeat for additional cycles
8. Click 'Start Method' when ready

1.question: Why is my baseline noisy or unstable?
1.answer:
Noisy or unstable baselines can have several causes:

**Common Causes:**
1. **Air bubbles** - Degas buffers, check tubing connections
2. **Temperature fluctuations** - Allow 30min thermal equilibration
3. **Flow rate issues** - Verify pump operation, check for blockages
4. **Contaminated flow cell** - Run cleaning protocol
5. **Poor calibration** - Recalibrate detector (Settings → OEM LED Calibration)

**Quick Fixes:**
• Prime system with fresh, degassed buffer
• Reduce flow rate temporarily
• Apply light smoothing filter (Graphic Display tab)
• Run baseline capture after stabilization

If noise persists after these steps, contact support.

2.question: Can I use Affilabs without connecting hardware?
2.answer:
Yes! Affilabs can be used without hardware for several purposes:

**Available Without Hardware:**
• View and analyze previous data files
• Learn the software interface
• Set up user profiles and preferences
• Explore data analysis tools
• Export existing data to different formats
• Review help documentation
• Plan experimental methods

**Requires Hardware Connection:**
• Live data acquisition
• Calibration procedures
• Real-time measurements
• Pump and valve control
• Temperature monitoring

This makes Affilabs great for training and data review even when instruments aren't available.

3.question: How do I install Affilabs?
3.answer:
Installing Affilabs is straightforward:

**Installation Steps:**
1. Download Affilabs installer from Affinité Instruments website
2. Run the installer as Administrator
3. Follow the setup wizard prompts
4. Accept license agreement
5. Choose installation directory (default recommended)
6. Complete installation

**After Installation:**
• Create desktop shortcut if desired
• Launch Affilabs
• Set up user profile
• Configure data storage locations

**System Requirements:**
• Windows 10 or later
• 4GB RAM minimum
• 2GB free disk space
• Administrator privileges for installation

Contact support if you encounter installation issues.

4.question: What does SPR measure?
4.answer:
Surface Plasmon Resonance (SPR) measures biomolecular interactions without labels:

**Primary Measurements:**
• **Binding Events** - When molecules bind to a sensor surface
• **Kinetics** - Association and dissociation rates
• **Affinity** - Strength of molecular interactions
• **Concentration** - Amount of analyte present
• **Specificity** - Selectivity between different molecules

**Common Applications:**
• Protein-protein interactions
• Drug discovery and screening
• Antibody characterization
• Quality control testing
• Biosensor development

**Advantages:**
• Real-time monitoring
• No labeling required
• Quantitative results
• Small sample volumes
• Fast analysis

SPR is ideal for studying how biological molecules interact with each other.

5.question: Where do I create methods?
5.answer:
Methods are created in the right sidebar of Affilabs:

**Location:**
• Right sidebar → Method tab (flask icon)
• Look for the '+ Add Cycle' button

**Step-by-Step:**
1. Click the Method tab on the right side
2. Click '+ Add Cycle' to start building
3. Choose cycle type (Baseline, Association, etc.)
4. Set duration and parameters
5. Name your cycle descriptively
6. Click 'Add' to add to queue
7. Repeat for additional cycles
8. Click 'Start Method' when ready

**Method Structure:**
A method is a sequence of cycles that defines your experiment:
• Baseline → Association → Dissociation → Regeneration

**Tip:** Start with a simple 3-cycle method: Baseline (5 min) → Association (10 min) → Dissociation (10 min)
